jokes.text: baby Ben got to be baby Jesus in our church's nativity play. Andre was Joseph, and Jordan was one of the wise men, and I was in charge of casting.
jokes.setup: Shirley describes her family's roles in nativity play
jokes.punchline: The implication that she cast her own family in all the good roles
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted character moment showing Shirley's self-serving nature disguised as religious participation

jokes.text: I digitized my entire movie collection. Goodbye, VHS. Hello, SelectaVision CED Videodisc.
jokes.setup: Abed announces digitizing his collection, suggesting modern upgrade
jokes.punchline: He's upgrading to an even more obsolete format than VHS
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed joke - shows his obsessive media knowledge while subverting expectations about technological progress

jokes.text: I'm very psyched for the new semester. Or should I say... Intro to Human Psyched.
jokes.setup: Annie expresses excitement for the semester
jokes.punchline: Forced pun combining 'psyched' with psychology class name
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that relies on wordplay without much cleverness, though it fits Annie's earnest character

jokes.text: The worst.
jokes.setup: Annie's enthusiastic pun about psychology
jokes.punchline: Jeff's flat dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - Jeff's deadpan reaction to Annie's earnestness is classic character dynamic

jokes.text: Don't you do way too much of that already?
jokes.setup: Annie mentions diagnosing fellow students for psychology
jokes.punchline: Troy points out she already does this constantly
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Annie's tendency to psychoanalyze everyone

jokes.text: Accusational opposition disorder.
jokes.setup: Troy questions Annie's diagnostic tendencies
jokes.punchline: Britta immediately diagnoses Troy with made-up disorder
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Britta doing exactly what Troy accused Annie of, with a cleverly made-up disorder name

jokes.text: It appears you've all noticed that my swagger has a new swagger.
jokes.setup: Jeff announces something has changed about him
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous recursive phrase about swagger having swagger
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jeff's narcissistic verbosity with an absurd but memorable phrase

jokes.text: I explained that really well.
jokes.setup: Jeff rambles about his therapy and medication
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory comment about his own explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff narcissism - complimenting himself on basic communication

jokes.text: I'm an exceptional narcissist, Britta.
jokes.setup: Britta calls Jeff a textbook narcissist
jokes.punchline: Jeff takes pride in being exceptional at narcissism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character joke - Jeff literally takes pride in the criticism, proving Britta's point perfectly

jokes.text: I didn't kill my wife! / I don't care.
jokes.setup: Celebrity impersonators acting out The Fugitive in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Random movie scene playing out in community college
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual establishing the celebrity impersonator premise with recognizable movie dialogue

jokes.text: He spent New Year's locked in our bathroom with a bad Tom Hanks from Cast Away. / Abed was the volleyball?
jokes.setup: Troy describes Abed's impersonator obsession
jokes.punchline: Annie's confusion about who played which role
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the absurdity - the image of Abed as Wilson the volleyball is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Chang, they're just acting!
jokes.setup: Chang appears to be tackling or attacking the impersonators
jokes.punchline: He doesn't realize they're just acting out movie scenes
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang confusion moment - his security guard role clashing with reality

jokes.text: I wouldn't do that. I have a heavy flashlight for them.
jokes.setup: Dean asks about Chang using tranquilizer gun on small students
jokes.punchline: Chang's solution is different weapon for different sized students
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Chang's inappropriate security methods, showing his twisted logic

jokes.text: We now get 80% of our electricity from the apartment building across the street.
jokes.setup: Dean explains the school's financial problems
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous solution of stealing electricity from neighbors
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail about the school's desperate financial state

jokes.text: I don't know who told you pouting was an option for you, but all you're making me feel right now is hatred of Renee Zellweger.
jokes.setup: Chang is pouting about not getting security guards
jokes.punchline: Dean compares Chang's expression to Renee Zellweger's signature pouty face
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected comparison that's both mean and accurate

jokes.text: You won't regret this unless I rise up against you. I don't know why I said that. Bye.
jokes.setup: Chang thanks Dean for letting him hire student security
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals potential rebellion plans then gets confused by his own words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - accidentally reveals his schemes then gets confused by his own threatening words

jokes.text: Huh! Tah! Zhou! Zeet! Dah! Toi!
jokes.setup: Chang exits the Dean's office
jokes.punchline: Makes martial arts sounds while presumably doing moves
jokes.explanation: Standard Chang physical comedy - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, that thing about Danny Thomas? I looked it up too. Weird.
jokes.setup: Britta says she looked something up and was right
jokes.punchline: Jeff assumes she means something completely different
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jeff's narcissistic assumption that everything relates to him

jokes.text: Okay, look. This apple is your ego. Imagine it expanding to the point of critical mass, taking over your whole personality, making you an uncontrollable monster.
jokes.setup: Britta tries to explain Jeff's condition with visual aid
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous attempt to demonstrate psychological concept with fruit
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta pseudo-psychology moment with absurd visual demonstration

jokes.text: Britta, how did an apple make that clear? Imagine it expanding? Use a balloon.
jokes.setup: Britta's confusing apple metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jeff logically points out the obvious flaw in her demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good logical comeback that deflates Britta's pseudo-intellectual moment

jokes.text: Thank you, Leonard, for that compliment, and for your service to this country.
jokes.setup: Leonard gives Jeff a simple compliment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-the-top gracious response including military service reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's medication-induced overconfidence through excessively gracious response

jokes.text: Final boarding call, beefcake airways.
jokes.setup: Jeff puts on aviator sunglasses
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous airline announcement about his own attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Jeff's narcissism with memorable absurd phrase

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Even his shadow! Look at his shadow!
jokes.setup: Jeff walks by in sunglasses looking confident
jokes.punchline: Dean is overwhelmed by Jeff's attractiveness, even his shadow
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the Dean's attraction to Jeff - even his shadow is appealing

jokes.text: French Stewart. But I don't have three grand.
jokes.setup: Vinnie says Abed resembles French Stewart and talks about gold mines running dry
jokes.punchline: Abed's flat acknowledgment while admitting he's broke
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed deadpan response to absurd situation

jokes.text: Holy crap, you'd make an amazing Seacrest.
jokes.setup: Vinnie is threatening Abed about the debt
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by Jeff's resemblance to Ryan Seacrest
jokes.explanation: Good plot device moment that advances the story while being mildly funny

jokes.text: You're actually more handsome than the guy that's famous for being handsome.
jokes.setup: Vinnie compliments Jeff's resemblance to Seacrest
jokes.punchline: Meta observation about being more attractive than someone famous for attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Clever meta observation about celebrity culture and attractiveness hierarchy

jokes.text: Got an Oprah. And sitting next to her is a Judy Garland or an Anne Hathaway. Add a few extra teeth.
jokes.setup: Vinnie evaluates the group for celebrity resemblances
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Annie's teeth
jokes.explanation: Mild character roast that advances the impersonator premise

jokes.text: Fat Brando. / Burt Reynolds. / What? / What?
jokes.setup: Vinnie identifies Troy and Shirley as celebrity lookalikes
jokes.punchline: Conflicting identifications leading to mutual confusion
jokes.explanation: Good quick visual gag about confusing celebrity identifications

jokes.text: Next time it'll be $10,000, and then $20,000, and then he'll hit rock bottom and have no one to turn to but Jesus. I say we let this play out.
jokes.setup: Group discussing whether to help Abed with his debt
jokes.punchline: Shirley wants to let Abed suffer so he'll find religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley moment - using religious logic to justify letting friend suffer

jokes.text: Abed is a magical, elf-like man who makes us all more magical by being near us.
jokes.setup: Pierce argues they should help Abed
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously romantic description of Abed
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd Pierce moment with unexpectedly poetic language about Abed

jokes.text: Who got you a DVD of Precious, based on the novel Push, by Sapphire, and a copy of the novel Push autographed by Sapphire?
jokes.setup: Pierce listing things Abed has done for them
jokes.punchline: Overly specific description of the Precious gift
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with overly specific movie title that becomes absurd through repetition

jokes.text: Fat Brando. / Burt Reynolds.
jokes.setup: Callback to earlier celebrity identification confusion
jokes.punchline: Same disagreement repeated
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that maintains the running gag without adding much

jokes.text: I send two guys that look a lot like Ving Rhames and Michael Chiklis. And they do a very convincing impression of breaking your friend's legs. Their secret is... They actually break them.
jokes.setup: Vinnie threatens consequences if they mess up the gig
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'impersonators' actually do what they're impersonating
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation with perfect timing on the reveal

jokes.text: I wouldn't want him next to me in a foxhole.
jokes.setup: Vinnie describes the Bar Mitzvah boy
jokes.punchline: Military metaphor about a 13-year-old's reliability
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Vinnie's tough-guy persona applied to child

jokes.text: Aw, zip it, white Jacko.
jokes.setup: Britta warns Jeff not to wear the tux
jokes.punchline: Jeff dismisses her while identifying her as white Michael Jackson
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff line showing his medication-induced confidence and casual dismissiveness

jokes.text: If you have anything else to say, say it in a high-pitched voice while walking backwards.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismisses Britta's concerns
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous condition for her to continue speaking
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Jeff power move that shows his inflated ego through ridiculous demands

jokes.text: Jeff is in grave danger. Hee-hee!
jokes.setup: Jeff's ridiculous demand that she speak in high voice walking backwards
jokes.punchline: Britta actually does it while delivering warning
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Britta's desperation and commitment to warning them

jokes.text: Which one? / Do I have to say it?
jokes.setup: Shirley says she's one of the impersonators
jokes.punchline: Bouncer's reluctance to identify which celebrity she resembles
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment about appearance-based casting awkwardness

jokes.text: Burt Reynolds. / Why not? / Do I have to say it?
jokes.setup: Troy identifies as Burt Reynolds impersonator
jokes.punchline: Same reluctant response from bouncer
jokes.explanation: Continues the visual gag about casting inappropriateness

jokes.text: I'm Brando. / Could be under 'fat.' / Totally.
jokes.setup: Shirley identifies as Brando impersonator
jokes.punchline: Bouncer finds her under 'fat' category, then agrees enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Escalates the casting awkwardness with bouncer's blunt honesty

jokes.text: I was so sad to hear of your passing. / Me too.
jokes.setup: Abed talking to Lorenzo Lamas impersonator
jokes.punchline: Impersonator agrees with condolences about his own death
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd exchange about celebrity death with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: What movie were Jamie Lee Curtis and Lorenzo Lamas in together? / See, now I'm just embarrassed for you because you've never seen Lorenzo's Oil.
jokes.setup: Chang questions the movie Abed is reenacting
jokes.punchline: Abed confidently describes completely fake movie with absurd plot
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Abed moment - confidently improvising ridiculous fake movie plot while maintaining character authority

jokes.text: Lorenzo is an oil tycoon that gets his address book switched with Jamie Lee Curtis', and they give each other piggyback rides.
jokes.setup: Abed describing the fake Lorenzo's Oil movie
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous plot involving piggyback rides
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the fake movie with increasingly absurd details delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: I can't believe... did you go to college? / Yes. No. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Chang is flummoxed by Abed's confident nonsense
jokes.punchline: Abed's casual dismissal of Chang's question
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up showing Abed's casual disregard for authority and facts

jokes.text: It's amazing, you're actually more handsome than the guy who's famous for being handsome.
jokes.setup: Continued compliments about Jeff's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Exact same line Vinnie used earlier
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that reinforces Jeff's ego inflation

jokes.text: You look like a friend of mine. / You're friends with Moby?
jokes.setup: Kid tells Annie she looks familiar
jokes.punchline: Annie's surprised reaction to being compared to Moby
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Annie's resemblance to the bald electronic musician

jokes.text: You have bad posture. When you do too many push-ups, it looks like you have boobs. The tile in your bathroom is tacky. You were emotionally closed off in bed to the point where one time, I didn't come up because I couldn't find close enough parking. You have an unusually high butt crack.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks Britta to deflate his ego by insulting him
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of increasingly specific and personal insults
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with increasingly intimate and specific criticisms, perfect Britta character moment

jokes.text: You have an unusually high butt crack.
jokes.setup: Britta continues listing Jeff's flaws
jokes.punchline: Ends with oddly specific anatomical criticism
jokes.explanation: Great capper to the escalation - unexpectedly specific and awkward physical detail. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Thank you. It couldn't have been easy for you to lie like that.
jokes.setup: Britta delivers harsh but truthful criticisms
jokes.punchline: Jeff assumes she was lying because he can't accept criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jeff's complete inability to process criticism even when he requested it

jokes.text: There's awards?
jokes.setup: Guest mentions awards at the party
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate interest despite trying to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment showing his inability to resist validation even when trying to be good

jokes.text: Nah. Oh, give me a break. It's his Bar Mitzvah. / There's an award for most handsome young man!
jokes.setup: Jeff initially dismisses winning the handsome award at a kid's party
jokes.punchline: Immediately changes his mind when he hears there's an actual award
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jeff's instant capitulation to ego when awards are involved

jokes.text: Nooo!
jokes.setup: Howie Schwartz wins the handsome young man award
jokes.punchline: Jeff's devastated reaction to losing to a 13-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing Jeff's complete meltdown over losing to a child at his own Bar Mitzvah

jokes.text: Look at me! Look at meee!
jokes.setup: Jeff's ego medication causes complete breakdown
jokes.punchline: He demands attention while presumably hulking out
jokes.explanation: Excellent culmination of Jeff's narcissistic breakdown with pure ego demand

jokes.text: I love you, dad! I knew you'd invite the Hulk!
jokes.setup: Jeff has destroyed the Bar Mitzvah party
jokes.punchline: Birthday boy thinks the Hulk appearance was planned entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment where Jeff's breakdown is interpreted as entertainment by the kid

jokes.text: Boogie Nights. / You're fat Burt Reynolds, right? / I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Troy references the movie to prove his Burt Reynolds credentials
jokes.punchline: Vinnie's blunt categorization and Troy's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good resolution of the running gag with Troy's pragmatic acceptance

jokes.text: It's your femur bone. He needs some money. He's broke. Ha ha ha. Hey. Laughter's the best medicine.
jokes.setup: Abed has hired impersonator to cheer up his broken leg
jokes.punchline: Patch Adams delivering terrible medical-themed comedy
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Abed's continued impersonator obsession despite consequences

jokes.text: So we just spent our whole night paying off your debt, and you're blowing money on a Patch Adams?
jokes.setup: Group discovers Abed hired another impersonator
jokes.punchline: Troy's exasperated pointing out of the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man moment highlighting the circular nature of Abed's problem

jokes.text: It's two for one. We're doing Popeye next. Ah-ga-ga-ga-ga. Get him some spinach. I'm Olive Oyl.
jokes.setup: Abed justifies hiring more impersonators
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's getting multiple characters and does Popeye impression
jokes.explanation: Good Abed escalation showing his complete inability to learn from consequences

jokes.text: Then I guess you're gonna have to trust that you're gonna have to trust me.
jokes.setup: Abed says he doesn't know if he can trust Troy to know better
jokes.punchline: Troy's recursive logic about trust
jokes.explanation: Good Troy moment with his characteristic circular logic delivered with sincerity

jokes.text: You know, Seacrest Hulk, you're a meshugeneh.
jokes.setup: Continued celebration of Seacrest Hulk
jokes.punchline: Jewish term of endearment for the ridiculous hybrid character
jokes.explanation: Good cultural detail that grounds the absurdist moment in the Bar Mitzvah setting

jokes.text: Maybe that should be me. / You are way out of my league, diagnostically speaking. I'm gonna go with someone a little less complicated. Like Abed.
jokes.setup: Jeff offers to be Britta's psychology study subject
jokes.punchline: Britta considers Abed less complicated than Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Abed, the most complex character, is seen as simpler than narcissistic Jeff

jokes.text: You can drive faster, change direction. And the only pee breaks are yours. / Are you real? / Are you?
jokes.setup: Abed talks to his mirror/evil twin about traveling alone
jokes.punchline: Philosophical question about reality between the two Abeds
jokes.explanation: Meta-textual brilliance - Abed questioning reality with his evil twin touches on the show's core themes

jokes.text: This is really crazy. And inaccessible, and maybe too dark. / Maybe to them, but not to us.
jokes.setup: Abed talking to his reflection about their situation
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on the show's experimental nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Abed directly addresses the show's experimental, inaccessible nature while talking to himself

jokes.text: Who's ready to chang the world? Chang! Chang! Chang! All hail Chang!
jokes.setup: Chang addressing his new student security force
jokes.punchline: Name-based pun and cult-like chanting
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang pun with escalation to dictator-like worship, setting up future storylines
